howItWorks.intro: This degrees to radians converter uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How do I convert degrees to radians?
faq.answer: Multiply the degree value by π/180. For example, 90° × π/180 = π/2 radians. Common conversions: 30° = π/6, 45° = π/4, 60° = π/3, 90° = π/2, 180° = π, 360° = 2π.

faq.question: How do I convert radians to degrees?
faq.answer: Multiply the radian value by 180/π. For example, π/3 × 180/π = 60°.

faq.question: Why do we use radians in math?
faq.answer: Radians are the natural unit for angles in calculus and higher math because they simplify formulas. For instance, the derivative of sin(x) is cos(x) only when x is in radians. Arc length also simplifies to s = rθ.

faq.question: What is a radian?
faq.answer: A radian is the angle subtended at the center of a circle by an arc whose length equals the radius. One full revolution = 2π radians ≈ 6.2832 radians.
